title: Repository Portability and Production Contracts Implementation Tasks
principles: Keep the rollout website-agnostic: do not change the current docs or web applications, their routes, identity, design, accessibility, performance or deployment.
Adopt TaxKit-owned contracts and evidence rather than copying another repository's source, names, paths or complete configuration.
Use owner-named Schema contracts and schema-derived types; do not mechanically brand strings or create a generic schema bucket.
Add static rules only when exact scopes and real Oxlint binary fixtures prove a reliable signal without broad suppressions.
Implement one task at a time with parent review, verification and acceptance before the next task begins.

decisionBlockers.decision: Choose whether the exported IsoDate Schema gains runtime calendar-date validation, is replaced through a compatibility/deprecation path, or is documented as constructor-validated only.
evidence: packages/core/src/primitives/date.ts documents validation but applies only Schema.brand to Schema.String
effect@4.0.0-beta.98 documents that Schema.brand adds no runtime checks

decisionBlockers.decision: Choose whether @taxkit/calculators joins the Changesets fixed group or remains independently versioned.
evidence: The strict downstream validator and repository status treat calculators as the ninth 1.0.0 artifact
.changeset/config.json and docs/standards/versioning.md define an eight-package fixed group that omits calculators

decisionBlockers.decision: Choose whether public rulePackVersion fields report owner package semver, a distinct stable rule-pack identifier, or follow a compatibility/deprecation path.
evidence: packages/rules/au/income-tax/src/calculator/annual-tax.ts emits rules-au-income-tax/0.0.0 while its manifest is 1.0.0; this mismatch is evidence only and does not select package semver
packages/rules/au/pay/src/calculator/take-home-pay.ts emits rules-au-pay/0.0.0 while its manifest is 1.0.0; changing the public encoded value risks exact-value consumers and snapshots

globalVerification.commitPolicy: Commit each coherent task only after its mandatory gates pass, three documented improvement audits are accepted, the Changeset decision is reviewed, and the parent confirms the task still matches the spec call graphs.
evidenceRequired: Zero tracked machine-local checkout references across every Git-tracked readable text file
Final repository-path, string-contract, portable-lint and production-Knip call graphs
String-contract audit summary by owning package or app boundary
Accepted, rejected, alias and unrelated-shadow real-binary evidence for every new portable lint rule
Production Knip entrypoint inventory and exact rationale for every exception
Approved STR-DEC-001, STR-DEC-002 and REL-DEC-001 decision records before final rollout acceptance
Three documented improvement audit passes for every substantial task
Changeset path and release-train impact, or explicit no-Changeset rationale for each task

tasks.title: Remove machine-local references and enforce repository path hygiene
tasks.goal: Make all tracked source and documentation portable across clean checkouts and prevent workstation-specific checkout paths from returning.
scope: README.md and the 10 other completed-plan/spec/task-list files in the 2026-07-18 baseline path audit
A focused root repository-path policy and runtime under tools/repository-paths
Root command wiring and canonical verification
Architecture, testing and root README guidance required by the new gate
outputs: Repository-relative or repository-identity replacements for all 39 baseline references
Schema-backed path findings and typed command failures
Deterministic accepted and rejected path-check tests
Canonical check:repository-paths and check:repository-paths:types root commands included in verification
A canonical test:repository-paths root command included in the root test graph
Updated root README, package-ownership, tooling and testing/quality documentation
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the TaxKit spec/task list.

Before editing, read the target spec, this task object, relevant current files, and the relevant docs in docs/architecture/*. Identify the owner for every schema, type, service, layer and tagged error you touch. Review the spec call graphs before editing and report any final difference.

MUST use Effect-native primitives and platform APIs when they fit, including Schema, Array, Match, Context, Layer, Config, Record, Result, Exit, Bun, Platform, ChildProcess and ChildProcessSpawner. Do not use nullable values, mutable records, switch, Object enumeration, ad hoc tags, unsafe casts, custom process handling or hand-rolled runtime wrappers when an Effect primitive fits. Reuse canonical schemas, derived types, tagged errors, services and constructors. Do not create mirrored DTOs, generic file-scanner abstractions or one-use mapper/error helpers. Keep the primary repository check as one meaningful linear Effect pipeline or Effect.gen program; map typed failures inline at the owning boundary.

Task-specific: remove all 39 baseline references from the 11 files named in the spec without deleting useful history. Implement the root-owned tools/repository-paths/schemas.ts, policy.ts, check.runtime.ts, focused tests and tsconfig.json only. Use the repository's Effect v4 ChildProcess/ChildProcessSpawner model for the NUL-delimited git ls-files inventory and Effect FileSystem/Path for reads. Scan every Git-tracked readable text file with bounded concurrency. Treat NUL-containing or strict-UTF-8-invalid payloads as binary. Cover the five closed path categories and accepted relative/repository/HTTPS/prose cases, including an accepted portable tilde-dot tool-state path. Findings contain only repository-relative file, positive line and category and sort by file/line/category; never render matched text, username, process stderr or surrounding content. Build rejected test values from neutral fragments so the checker/tests pass their own scan. Add check:repository-paths and check:repository-paths:types to verification and test:repository-paths to root test. Keep @taxkit/scripts unchanged. Add no service/layer or helper unless it proves a substitution point beyond pure policy and CLI integration.

Run every mandatory verification gate and document at least three improvement audit passes. Report changed files, verification outcomes, residual risks, the final call graph and the internal-tool no-Changeset rationale unless a package-facing contract changes. Do not start or delegate another task. The parent must review, verify and accept this task before the next task begins.
mandatoryVerification: bun run test:repository-paths
bun run check:repository-paths:types
bun run check:repository-paths
Independent git-tracked readable-text audit confirming the 39-reference baseline is zero and the checker/tests are in scope
bun run verification
bun run changeset status --verbose and an explicit no-Changeset rationale unless a public package contract changed

Effect audit: one meaningful linear primary program, typed errors mapped at the owning boundary, canonical Schema-derived findings, no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual object readers or trivial wrappers
Import audit confirming no browser or runtime surface gained a server-only dependency

conditional: bun.lock only if the root Effect Platform dependency surface changes
notApplicable: packages/scripts/** because @taxkit/scripts remains orchestration-only
.github/workflows/quality.yml because it already invokes root verification
apps/docs/**, apps/web/** and browser proof

qualityAudits: Pass 1: inspect matching policy, exact exclusions and fixtures for false positives, false negatives and workstation-specific assumptions.
Pass 2: inspect the command call graph, Effect control flow, schema-derived findings, typed failures, unsafe casts, DTO mirrors and helper sprawl.
Pass 3: inspect all replacements for preserved historical meaning, relative-link correctness, root verification wiring and zero residual tracked references.
completionCriteria: The parent independently confirms zero tracked machine-local checkout references.
The path check covers every Git-tracked readable text file and reports safe schema-backed locations.
Generated, binary or vendored exclusions are exact, documented and tested.
Runtime-assembled rejected cases fail focused tests, and root verification passes with the tool and tests included in the clean repository scan.
Root verification typechecks and runs the path scan, while root test runs the focused path suite so CI enforces both contracts.
The parent accepts all three audit passes, final call graph and Changeset decision before the next task.

tasks.title: Define and audit the canonical string-contract taxonomy
tasks.goal: Define the selection policy and produce a decision-ready audit of package-facing, configured, persisted, diagnostic and provider-facing string contracts without changing package behavior.

@taxkit/scripts command evidence and apps/api configuration/runtime contracts

STR-DEC-001, STR-DEC-002 and REL-DEC-001 evidence plus every additional compatibility decision discovered by the audit
outputs: Durable string-contract taxonomy in canonical architecture or standards guidance
An implementation-evidence inventory of audited owners and decisions

No package contract or runtime behavior changes in this audit slice
An explicit documentation-only no-Changeset rationale
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one audit/documentation task from the TaxKit spec/task list.

Before editing, read the target spec, this task object, package manifests/exports, relevant schemas/config/persistence/transport/diagnostic files, and the relevant docs in docs/architecture/*. Identify the owning package for every Schema, type, ID, constructor, service, layer and tagged error in the ledger. Review the spec call graphs before editing and report any final difference.

Use installed Effect 4.0.0-beta.98 semantics: Schema.brand is nominal and adds no runtime check; raw secret ingress uses RedactedFromValue while Schema.Redacted expects an already-redacted value. Plain string or inline Schema.String/NonEmptyString is correct for ordinary content without independent validation, secrecy, mixing or reuse policy. Do not propose generic brand factories, content-schema wrappers, shared schema buckets, unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers, repeated internal decoding, stringly branching or trivial helpers. Preserve legitimate explicit recursive encoded types and generic descriptor interfaces.

Task-specific: update docs/architecture/effect-services.md as canonical taxonomy owner and keep docs/standards/code-patterns.md to concise linked examples. In the active execution plan, inventory only string-shaped fields in exported Schemas/types/constructor inputs/configuration/encoded or diagnostic contracts reachable from the nine artifact manifests, plus @taxkit/scripts and apps/api. Do not inventory every literal, tag, label, fixture or env-key. Each row records owning module/Schema, field, exposure boundary, current/proposed category, runtime constraints, brand identity, Type, Encoded, constructor path, ingress decode, egress encode, compatibility and keep/change/N/A. Record @taxkit/tsconfig application strings N/A, absent persistence/provider/secret surfaces N/A, STR-DEC-001, STR-DEC-002, REL-DEC-001, scripts cwd/stdout/stderr rendering and SDK Cause.pretty diagnostics. For each proposed correction name exact files, focused owner commands, package-facing Changeset impact and apps/api CHANGELOG impact. Do not change package code, manifests, Changesets config or runtime behavior in this task.

Run every mandatory gate and document three improvement audit passes. Report the ledger, decisions, changed docs, residual risks, call-graph alignment and documentation-only no-Changeset rationale. Do not start or delegate another task. The parent must review and accept this audit before the next task begins.
mandatoryVerification: Complete manifest-to-field ledger with every column and exclusion named in the task prompt
Evidence that STR-DEC-001, STR-DEC-002 and REL-DEC-001 remain explicit blockers rather than implicit code or release-policy changes

Audit proving brand-only versus runtime-checked schemas are distinguished, Redacted variants match ingress representation, closed vocabularies use literals, and ordinary content does not create named-schema sprawl
Audit of scripts cwd/stdout/stderr and SDK Cause.pretty diagnostic egress with a proposed sentinel test or exact local non-persistent policy

Decision-ready exact owner commands: check-types/test/build; calculators and SDK test-types; API HTTP test:openapi; SDK check-boundaries/packed/downstream when its package graph changes; API app check-types/build/smoke; scripts check-types/test/build
bun run verification
Explicit documentation-only no-Changeset rationale
bun run changeset status --verbose

change: the active execution plan audit ledger

owner package source, manifests, tests, READMEs, Changesets and apps/api/CHANGELOG.md in this audit-only task

qualityAudits: Pass 1: challenge every proposed brand, check, literal or named content contract for semantic value, correct owner, compatibility and schema/helper sprawl.
Pass 2: inspect Type/Encoded, constructor, ingress/egress and diagnostic data flow; distinguish canonical recursion/generic interfaces from DTO mirrors.
Pass 3: inspect manifest coverage, explicit N/A rows, exact correction commands/artifacts, decision blockers, docs ownership and no-Changeset accuracy.
completionCriteria: Canonical guidance defines all six string-contract categories and when not to brand.

STR-DEC-001, STR-DEC-002, REL-DEC-001 and any additional breaking correction are reported as decision blockers.

tasks.title: Select and enforce additional portable Effect lint contracts

scope: tools/oxlint/effect-rules.js and shared binding analysis
Portable accepted, rejected and unrelated-shadow fixtures
Exact rule scopes and boundary files in oxlint.config.ts
Necessary migrations in existing non-website Effect code
Effect and testing/quality architecture guidance
outputs: A recorded admit/reject decision for each candidate portable rule
Binding-aware implementations for admitted rules
Visitor tests and installed-Oxlint binary fixtures
Exact enabled scopes with no broad ignores or inline suppressions
Focused source migrations and aligned architecture documentation
tasks.implementationPrompt:
You are implementing exactly one task from the TaxKit spec/task list.

Before editing, read the target spec, this task object, current Oxlint plugins/config/fixtures, affected source, and the relevant docs in docs/architecture/*. Identify every canonical binding, boundary allowlist, owning schema/type/service/layer/error and current rule overlap. Review the spec call graphs before editing and report any final difference.

MUST implement portable rules with binding-aware AST analysis that resolves canonical direct/namespace imports, renamed bindings, statically known destructuring and reassignment while accepting unrelated shadowed locals. Use current shared binding utilities when they fit. Keep one-off source migrations as direct Effect pipes or Effect.gen programs with typed errors handled inline at the owning boundary. Reuse canonical schemas and derived types. Do not duplicate the exact decoder rule, move route/browser policy into the portable namespace, add broad globs/suppressions, use text-only lint heuristics, introduce unsafe casts/DTO mirrors/manual readers, or extract trivial rule/source helpers.

Task-specific: build the decision matrix before editing. Evaluate bare Effect.tryPromise, host API use in services, adapter execution without an explicit boundary, nullable boundary leakage, Result/Exit re-encoding, non-throwing synchronous decoder placement and any calculator rule that may be genuinely domain-neutral. For every candidate record exact candidate ID, existing overlap, intended scope, positive/negative cases, migration count and admit/reject reason. Explicitly reconcile effect/no-host-imports-in-contracts, effect/no-runtime-execution-outside-boundaries, bun/no-host-api-outside-adapters, bun/no-runtime-outside-entrypoints, effect/no-schema-encoder-outside-egress, effect/no-throwing-schema-sync-codec, taxkit/no-decoding-outside-boundaries and taxkit/no-nullish-comparison. Extend the current owner when semantics match and reject duplicates. Zero new rules is valid when the evidence proves overlap or an unreliable static signal. For admitted rules, add focused cases plus the aggregate installed-Oxlint fixtures. Do not add website or route rules.

Run every mandatory verification gate and document at least three improvement audit passes. Report rules admitted/rejected, changed files, fixture evidence, migrated violations, residual risks, final call-graph alignment and the Changeset or explicit no-Changeset rationale. Do not start or delegate another task. The parent must review, verify and accept this task before the next task begins.
mandatoryVerification: Candidate inventory showing existing overlap, static signal, intended exact scope and admit/reject decision
bun run test:oxlint
Real installed-Oxlint accepted, rejected, renamed/aliased and unrelated-shadow fixture evidence for every admitted rule with nested config disabled
bun run lint
Audit confirming no broad ignorePatterns, package-wide boundary exemptions or inline disable directives were added

Effect audit for every migrated violation: meaningful linear control flow, typed error handling at the owning boundary, canonical Schema/type reuse, no unsafe casts, DTO mirrors, manual object readers or trivial wrappers
Import audit confirming browser/runtime code still consumes browser-safe API and SDK exports
bun run verification
bun run changeset for package-facing behavior changes, or explicit tooling/internal no-Changeset rationale
bun run changeset status --verbose

change: focused rule test files for admitted rules

tools/oxlint/bun-rules.js or taxkit-rules.js only when the decision matrix deliberately changes existing ownership
owner source/tests/README and Changeset only for real migrated behavior
notApplicable: route-loader, website and browser rules
broad package exemptions, inline suppressions and disabled duplicate rules
.github/workflows/quality.yml because root verification already runs lint

qualityAudits: Pass 1: challenge each candidate for duplicate coverage, false positives, unresolved binding cases and whether review guidance is safer than lint.
Pass 2: inspect rule visitors, binding resolution, fixtures, exact scopes and source migrations for direct Effect-native control flow and canonical contracts.
Pass 3: inspect the full lint graph for namespace ownership, suppression pressure, decoder/route-boundary preservation, docs alignment and Changeset accuracy.
completionCriteria: Every admitted rule has a reliable binding-aware signal, exact scope and real-binary evidence.

TaxKit-specific decoder, tax and route rules remain separate and at least as strict as before.

tasks.implementationPrompt:

tasks.implementationPrompt:

Before asking for a decision, read the target spec, RPC-002's completed audit ledger and evidence, packages/core/src/primitives/date.ts, both rule report owners, installed Effect Schema.brand behavior, .changeset/config.json, docs/standards/versioning.md, the nine artifact manifests and strict downstream validation. Do not infer approval from repository state, prior version alignment or implementation convenience. Do not edit package code, manifests, Changesets configuration or runtime behavior before the user explicitly selects an option for STR-DEC-001, STR-DEC-002 and REL-DEC-001. If any decision is absent, keep this task and final rollout acceptance blocked.

After explicit approval, record the dated decisions in the spec and active plan. Replace RPC-006's unresolved scope with the exact owning files, commands, compatibility treatment, Changeset requirements and apps/api changelog impact identified by the audit and decisions. If a decision requires no code correction, record the exact documentation-only consequence and N/A rationale. tasks.implementationPrompt:

Use Schema.brand only for nominal identity with a separately enforced stable invariant when runtime validation is promised. Use RedactedFromValue for approved raw secret ingress and Schema.Redacted for already-redacted values. Keep ordinary content as plain string or inline Schema.String/NonEmptyString unless the decision ledger proves independent semantic policy. Preserve legitimate recursive encoded types and generic descriptors. Treat diagnostic safety as an egress data-flow contract and prove sentinel secret/private-path values cannot enter persistent or user-facing output unless an approved local-only policy says otherwise. Do not add generic brand factories, shared schema buckets, mirrored DTOs, unsafe casts, repeated defensive decoding, stringly branching or trivial helpers.
